content.full_text:
City of Pico Rivera
6615 PASSONS BOULEVARD • PICO RIVERA, CALIFORNIA 90660
P.O. BOX NO. 1016 • 213 942-2000 • 213 723-3191

September 19, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Attention: DeAnn Stone

Dear Ms. Stone:

As city Attorney for the City of Pico Rivera and Counsel for the
Pico Rivera Redevelopment Agency, I respectfully request
confirmation of your opinion rendered to me this date regarding the
matter hereinafter set forth. Your opinion was that the reasonably
foreseeable effects of the decisions would be material with respect
to the economic interests of all the public officials involved,
however the decisions would affect the interest of
Councilmen/Agency members, James Patronite, Rick Mercado, John
Chavez and Al Natividad in a manner which is not distinguishable
from the manner in which they would affect the public generally.
As such, they would not be disqualified from participating in the
subject decisions.

The members of the Pico Rivera City Council also serve as the Pico
Rivera Redevelopment Agency. Matters coming up for decision on
their part consist of the establishment of procedures for the
expansion of the membership of the existing Project Area Committee,
and the acceptance of a Preliminary Plan for an amendment to the
Redevelopment Project Area. The economic interests of the
councilmen/agency members is as follows:

1. James Patronite, Rick Mercado, and John Chavez each
reside in a single family residence within 300 feet of the proposed
expanded Project Area.

2. Al Natividad owns a single family rental within 300 feet
of the proposed expanded Project Area.

3. Garth Gardner owns a 141 unit apartment building within
the proposed amended Project Area.

The advice requested was whether the decision rendered would affect

[illegible] oofd to clip)

Fair Political Practices Commission
September 20, 1990
Page 2

the interests of the aforesaid officials in a manner which is not
distinguishable from the manner in which the decision will affect
the public generally.

The aforesaid decisions were scheduled to be acted upon at the City
Council/Agency meeting of September 17, 1990 but were continued
until the meeting of October 1, 1990 pending an opinion or advice
from the Fair Political Practices Commission.  October 1 is a
critical date as far as meeting the material deadlines.

I am aware that a longer period of time is generally allowed for
the rendering of advice or opinions, however, in view of the
critical nature of this situation, it would be greatly appreciated
if you could provide this office with the requested confirmation of
the opinion prior to the aforesaid meeting of October 1, 1990.

A compilation of the information and statistics which you requested
and was provided to you by telephonic communication is hereby
confirmed and set forth in memorandum attached hereto.

As I will be out of the office until September 27, 1990, please
direct all communications to Mr. Scott Nichols, Deputy City
Attorney at this office.

Your cooperation in this matter is greatly appreciated.

Sincerely,


SAMUEL SIEGEL, CITY ATTORNEY,
CITY OF PICO RIVERA
AGENCY COUNSEL, PICO RIVERA
REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY

SS:bh

City of Pico Rivera

6615 PASSONS BOULEVARD · PICO RIVERA, CALIFORNIA 90660
P.O. BOX NO. 1016 · 213 942-2000 · 213 723-3191

September 25, 1990


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Attention: Blanca Breeze

Dear Ms. Breeze:

In accordance with our telephone conversation this date, enclosed
please find the information that we discussed.

Sincerely,

SAMUEL SIEGEL, CITY ATTORNEY,

By [illegible]
Scott Nichols
Assistant City Attorney

SN:bh

Enclosure

[illegible] o-oi [illegible] [illegible]

INFORMATION REQUESTED BY MS. STONE
AND FURNISHED TELEPHONICALLY - 9/19/90


                        City-wide    Project      Project
                                     Area 1       Area 2

Area                    5,266.5      2151
                        acres        acres

Population              59,337       15,433

Homeowners-Single
Family Residence                                 3500

Rental Units with
Ownership by Those      1300                     612
Owing 3 or Fewer
Single Family
Residences

Lots of Residences of Councilmen as well as the Rental of one
Councilman are Comparable With Surrounding Lots


(1)    Existing and proposed project areas.

(2)    Existing and proposed project areas, plus area within 300
       feet radius.




                           ATTACHMENT
